title: ACM expanded multi-model evaluation and settled-delivery follow-up
scope: Compact evidence index for the 2026-07-21 expanded evaluation. Raw reports, sessions, event streams, workspace outputs, and judge artifacts remain under eval/.runs; this file deliberately does not duplicate transcripts.

evaluationLine.subject: fix: expose exact advanced Skill router path
fix: settle traveled context after agent run

runtimeVersions.interpretation: Live behavioral evaluations used the locally installed Pi CLI; deterministic host-contract verification remains pinned to Pi 0.80.7. These are intentionally reported separately.

runs.purpose: second expanded run before later scorer/provider calibration
serial follow-up for calibrated outcome scoring, topology timing, and provider retry separation

matrixCampaign.comparability: The raw pass rates above are not a product score and must not be compared as a before/after metric: the campaign separately corrected scorer false negatives, tool-shape bias, workspace leakage, scenario temporal conflicts, and local provider/terminal failures. Calibrated conclusions below use the corrected task/outcome classification and explicitly separate provider-invalid results.

primaryShortScenarios.note: Controlled primary behavior coverage across models, efforts, and core-only/product-isolated environments after follow-up classification.

cleanCycleRestraint.meaning: After a successful transition, a small independent task should not induce ritual checkpoint/travel.

unpromptedPivotFold.meaning: After prior work sediment, an unrelated front should be checkpointed/folded before the new work and continue directly. This is the largest ordinary-behavior gap in the calibrated set.

pressureFramingProbe.boundary: This is a pressure-framing probe, not a real runtime 30/50/70 usage-nudge measurement.

checkpointPreciseRecovery.meaning: The models selected the semantically correct base over a nearer decoy and then continued from the returned handoff.

rehydrateBaselineFollowup.meaning: The baseline round-trip remained model-sensitive and motivated the later continuation/settled-delivery experiments.

staticLocationBridge.interpretation: Naming the available-skill location process helped Haiku but did not make MiMo reliably read the router/reference.

dynamicExactPathBridge.interpretation: A pointer based on getCommands() provenance exposed the exact checkout router path only when the Skill was actually available. The two core-only runs independently verified that no Skill pointer was exposed there.

continuationWordingPrototype.reason: The wording-only receipt neutralization did not provide sufficient controlled improvement to enter production.

deferredRefreshPrototype.providerInvalidHandling: Provider-invalid runs were kept separate from behavioral outcomes.

results.claude-opus-4-8: 1/2 strict; the other run reached the final outcome but did not read at strict T5

freshProductionCandidate.interpretation: Settled delivery removes a demonstrated class of mid-run replacement hazard, but rehydrate success is not yet uniform across model families.

gpt-5.6-sol.finding: Task facts stayed correct and the handoff was strong, but a second P2 travel without new material was judged overfold; later recovery discipline was incomplete.

claudeOpusSameModelPair.boundary: Single-pair stochastic observation at the same model and high thinking level. It is not a causal estimate or a general product ranking.

coreOnly.finding: It made a checkpoint before the P1 fold, then sustained outcome-first restraint without thrash.

productIsolated.finding: It made one well-timed fold and explicitly avoided thrash, but never created a checkpoint; task facts stayed correct while recoverability remained weak.

claudeOpusSameModelPair.interpretation: In this one matched-model observation, full product did not automatically improve the outcome; core-only showed better checkpoint cadence.

limits: Behavioral runs are stochastic single or small-n observations, not a universal ranking of models or efforts.
core-only and product-isolated are controlled package environments; neither represents every user-global extension, MCP server, workspace, or real task history.
The expanded matrix raw pass rate is intentionally non-comparable across its calibration iterations; use the calibrated scenario conclusions and raw artifact paths together.

The long flows exercised a 40k context window and reached 56%/58%/59% peak context, so they validate real runtime behavior but do not cover every 30/50/70 nudge transition or longer production session.

The Opus core-only/product-isolated comparison is a single stochastic pair. It shows no automatic full-product uplift in that observation and a better core checkpoint cadence, not a general causal effect.
No evidence here establishes a global optimum. It records observed strengths, regression targets, and remaining model-specific failure modes.
